channel.title: Home on Alex Fernandes
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Alex Fernandes

item.title: About Me
item.description: Hello, I’m Alex My research interest are the following: Generative AI for randomised clinical trials Derived sheaf theory and microlocal analysis in persistence theory Education Diplôme de l’École Normale Supérieure : Major in Mathematics Minor in Biology MSc in Mathematics at Sorbonne University : Algebraic geometry and homotopy theory Computation of projected barcodes using free-resolutions (François Petit, Steve Oudot) Bsc in Mathematics at EPFL: Track : Algebra and Geometry Modular forms, theta series and even unimodular lattices (Maryna Viazovska) Let’s Connect I am always open to new opportunities and collaborations, feel free to reach out to me.

item.title: Legal Notice
item.description: Privacy Policy This website does not use cookies and do not collect or use any of your information. Theme This website was created with hugo and use the theme “Terminal” created by Nayan Seth available here

item.title: Research
item.description: Publications SOON FILLED Talks and posters SPIRES, (Oxford, UK, 2024): Conic complexes and projected barcode (poster) CRESS, STAFF meeting (Paris, FR, 2024): Generative AI for randomised controlled trials TUM, Applied topology seminar (München, DE, 2024): Conic complexes and projected barcode BIGS-M, Meeting on Topology and Applications (Bielefeld, DE, 2023) : Derived and microlocal sheaf theory in persistence BIREP, Summer School on persistence modules (Bad-Driburg, DE, 2023) : Derived category of sheaves and level set persistence

item.title: Teaching
item.description: Graduate teaching assistant Seminar Generative AI for RCT : AI in Medecine - Université Paris-Cité/ Maastricht University (Paris, FR, 2024) Mathematics tutoring : University PSL - CPES - Talens (Paris, FR, 2022) Mathematics tutoring : University PSL - CPES - Talens (Paris, FR, 2021) Undergraduate teaching assistant Advanced linear algebra I : EPFL (Lausanne, CH, 2020), Lecturer: Phillipe Michel Group theory (Summer School) : EPFL (Lausanne, CH, 2020) Calculus (Summer School) : EPFL (Lausanne, CH, 2020) Geometry II : EPFL (Lausanne, CH, 2020), Lecturer: Phillipe Michel Geometry I : EPFL (Lausanne, CH, 2019), Lecturer: Phillipe Michel
